summary: Iteration 4 frontend E2E re-verification on /hrms.html — BUG-FIX VERIFICATION FAILED. The DataTables CDN downgrade (v2.1.8 → v1.13.8, Buttons 3.1.2 → 2.4.2) did NOT resolve the regression. ROOT CAUSE is unrelated to the DataTables version: at /app/index.html line 435 the app declares `const $ = (s,p=document)=>p.querySelector(s);` which SHADOWS the jQuery `$` symbol inside the app's module scope. Therefore `$.fn.DataTable` → throws TypeError 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading DataTable)' and `$(tbl).DataTable(...)` → 'TypeError: $(...).DataTable is not a function'. Verified live: typeof $.fn.DataTable is 'function' on window (jQuery 3.7.1 + DataTables 1.13.8 ARE correctly loaded), but inside the app's evaluated code `$.fn` is undefined because the local `$` const overrides jQuery's `$`. As a direct consequence the KEY REGRESSION (tbody empty after Save) is NOT FIXED — Dashboard cards correctly show 'TOTAL EMPLOYEES: 3' (so STATE.data.employees has the rows) but #dt-employees tbody renders 0 <tr> elements. Same for Users (#dt-users) and Audit Logs (#dt-audit) — although Audit Logs has its own bespoke render path so it still shows 87 rows. No DataTable wrapper / search / pagination renders on any page.

ui_bugs.issue: After click Save, the .app-toast does NOT appear and #dt-employees tbody remains EMPTY (0 rows) even though Dashboard counter advances to 3 employees. Reproduces the KEY REGRESSION the iteration_4 fix was supposed to solve.

No .dataTables_wrapper / .dataTables_filter / .dataTables_paginate / .dt-buttons render on any of the 3 pages. Console throws on every navigation: 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading DataTable)' and '$(...).DataTable is not a function'. Search / sort / pagination / Export buttons are all absent.

integration_issues.issue: Local `const $ = (s,p=document)=>p.querySelector(s)` at line 435 of /app/index.html shadows jQuery `$` inside the IIFE / module scope. All subsequent calls to `$.fn.DataTable`, `$(tbl).DataTable(...)`, `$(tbl).html(...)` operate on the querySelector helper (which has no `.fn`, no `.html`, no `.DataTable`) and therefore throw. This is THE root cause — not the DataTables version.

verifications_passed: Login with admin@iaad.gov.in / Admin@12345 succeeds; #user-role badge reads 'super_admin' (iteration_3 role-elevation fix still works)
Sidebar still shows all nav items for super_admin including data-testid='nav-users' AND data-testid='nav-auditLogs' (no regression of iteration_3 fix)
Add Employee modal opens via [data-testid='add-employees-btn']; DOB 1985-03-20 → retirementDate auto-fills correctly to 2045-03-31 (last day of March 2045)

Save is NOT blocked — Firestore receives the record (Audit Logs page lists a fresh Create/employees entry with the new doc id and the Dashboard counter advances) — the regression is purely the front-end rendering, not the write path
jQuery 3.7.1, DataTables 1.13.8, Buttons 2.4.2 ARE correctly loaded on window (window.$.fn.DataTable === function, window.jQuery.fn.DataTable === function)
verifications_failed: KEY REGRESSION FIX FAIL: #dt-employees tbody empty (0 rows) after save; .app-toast 'Saved successfully' never appears; 'QA Test' not present in tbody
.dataTables_wrapper / .dataTables_filter / .dataTables_paginate missing on Employees, Users, Audit Logs
Console error '$(...).DataTable is not a function' is STILL fired every time renderTable() runs (Employees / Users) — only the message differs slightly because of the new error path
action_items: PRIMARY FIX (single-line change): in /app/index.html line 435/436 rename the local querySelector helper from `$` to something like `qs` (and `$$` → `qsa`), OR keep the helper but qualify every DataTables call with `window.jQuery(...).DataTable(...)` / `window.jQuery.fn.DataTable`. Recommended: rename the helper because the codebase already uses jQuery semantics ($(tbl).DataTable, $(`${tbl} tbody`).html(...)). Quickest patch: at line 435 replace `const $ = ...; const $$ = ...;` with an IIFE that does NOT shadow $ (e.g. `const qs = (s,p=document)=>p.querySelector(s); const qsAll = ...;`) and run a search-and-replace of standalone `$(` calls that are NOT jQuery (most uses already ARE jQuery — careful audit needed).

ALTERNATIVE FIX (zero search-and-replace): immediately above line 435 add `const _qs = (s,p=document)=>p.querySelector(s);` and `const _qsa = ...`, then DELETE the `const $` / `const $$` lines, then in the rest of the file replace ONLY the non-jQuery selector calls. Inspect every `$(` token: those that use ' #id' / '.class' / element strings are jQuery (keep as-is) — those that pass a CSS selector and immediately .addEventListener/.classList/.value are the helper (rename to _qs).
Revert the DataTables CDN downgrade back to v2.x is NOT required — 1.13.8 is fine and works on window scope. The downgrade itself is harmless but it did not address the actual bug.
Add a typeof $.fn.DataTable !== 'function' guard at top of renderTable() to fail loudly with a clearer message (defensive, not the fix).
critical_code_review_comments: HIGH: variable shadowing of jQuery `$` is a hard-to-spot bug; ANY page-script that mixes jQuery plugins (DataTables, Bootstrap modals via jQuery, etc.) with a local `$` helper will silently break. Document a code convention: never declare `$` / `$$` when jQuery is on the page.

MEDIUM: renderTable() swallows the DataTables exception — line 1012 `$(`${tbl} tbody`).html(...)` would itself throw because the local `$` returns a single DOM element (no .html method). Yet the page shows no visible toast/error. Add a global window.onerror toast in development mode.
MEDIUM: index.html is 1905 lines — split into modules (auth.js, modules.js, render.js, dt-init.js) so issues like this are easier to localise.

LOW: Firestore write succeeds even when UI render fails — confirms the bug is purely client-side. Good news, but means stale UI hides write success from the user — they will likely re-click Save and create duplicate records.

success_rate.frontend: 33% (2 of 6 review steps PASS: sidebar nav-items + retirement auto-calc; the 3 DataTable wrapper checks + the KEY add-employee regression all FAIL; sign-out not reachable because logout dropdown click failed due to missing UI state but app role/login itself works)

test_credentials: admin@iaad.gov.in / Admin@12345 — login works; role correctly self-heals to super_admin.
seed_data_creation: Multiple QA001 'QA Test' Create operations attempted during testing; Firestore audit log shows a fresh Create event but the row never rendered in #dt-employees tbody. Total employees counter advanced to 3 (was 2). No cleanup performed because the rows aren't visible in the UI to delete from the front-end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_4 fix (DataTables CDN downgrade) was applied but does NOT address the actual bug. Real cause: `const $ = (s,p=document)=>p.querySelector(s)` at line 435 of /app/index.html shadows jQuery's `$`. After the main agent applies the rename fix (helper → qs/qsa) OR qualifies DataTables calls with window.jQuery, re-run the full iteration_4 review_request — particularly (a) DataTable wrapper presence on Employees/Users/AuditLogs, (b) the Add Employee Save → toast → tbody row 'QA Test' visible regression, (c) console must be free of any '$(...).DataTable is not a function' or 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading DataTable)' errors. NOTE: jQuery, DataTables 1.13.8 and Buttons 2.4.2 ARE correctly loaded on window — no need to touch CDN tags.
rca_of_the_issue: ROOT CAUSE: Local `const $ = ...` helper at /app/index.html:435 shadows jQuery `$` inside the app scope. REPRODUCTION: open /hrms.html, sign in as admin@iaad.gov.in, navigate to Employees — observe 'TOTAL EMPLOYEES: 3' on Dashboard but '#dt-employees tbody' renders 0 rows; observe console PAGEERROR 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading DataTable)' fired by renderTable() line 1008; observe no .dataTables_wrapper / .dataTables_filter / .dataTables_paginate. EVIDENCE: in-page evaluate of `typeof $.fn.DataTable` on the login screen returns 'function' (libraries loaded) but the same expression inside renderTable's scope throws 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading DataTable)'. MITIGATION: rename the local `$` helper to `qs` (and `$$` → `qsa`) OR use `window.jQuery` / `jQuery` explicitly for every DataTables call; the DataTables CDN version is irrelevant to this bug — both 1.x and 2.x would fail identically given the shadowing.
